tier_field_semantics: The `tier` field on each step reflects the step's own test harness (Tier 1 = pure-Rust unit/proptest; Tier 2 = BPF_PROG_TEST_RUN; Tier 3 = real-kernel via Lima/ubuntu-latest; Tier 4 = veristat/xdp-perf gates), not the tier of the first scenario it unblocks. Newtype/enum steps are Tier 1 even when they unblock Tier 2/3 scenarios in subsequent steps of the same phase.

phases.name: Slice 01 — Real-iface XDP attach (veth, not lo)
phases.description: Lift Phase 2.1's xdp_pass from lo to a real veth0 pair inside Lima / ubuntu-latest. Lands S-2.2-01..03: real-veth attach + PACKET_COUNTER increment, structured generic-mode-fallback warning, typed IfaceNotFound error. No new BPF program or map; only iface-resolution + native-mode-default + structured-warning shape against a non-loopback driver. Implements ADR-0038 D8 (real-iface attach).
acceptance: S-2.2-01 Tier 3 — real veth pair attach + PACKET_COUNTER == 100 via bpftool map dump
S-2.2-02 Tier 3 — generic-mode fallback emits structured `xdp.attach.fallback_generic` warning
S-2.2-03 Tier 3 — missing iface returns DataplaneError::IfaceNotFound (no XDP loaded)

steps.name: Land iface-name resolution + IfaceNotFound typed error
criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
DataplaneError::IfaceNotFound { iface } variant exists
nix::net::if_::if_nametoindex resolves names
integration test asserts typed error on non-existent iface
steps.description: Replace the Phase 2.1 hardcoded-`lo` ifindex path with a name-based resolver in the EbpfDataplane loader. Adds the `IfaceNotFound { iface }` variant to `DataplaneError` (pass-through embedding rules) and routes resolution failures through it. Flips S-2.2-03 RED → GREEN.
acceptance_criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
DataplaneError::IfaceNotFound { iface: String } variant exists in overdrive-dataplane errors
Loader uses if_nametoindex and converts ENODEV/ENOENT to IfaceNotFound
Integration test S-2.2-03 asserts the typed variant via matches!
No regression on Phase 2.1 lo-attach test

steps.name: Native-mode default + structured fallback warning
criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
Loader attempts native XdpFlags first
on failure logs tracing::warn! `xdp.attach.fallback_generic` with iface field
falls back to generic
integration test asserts log
steps.description: Default `xdp.attach` to native flags. On EOPNOTSUPP / driver rejection emit a single structured `tracing::warn!` event named `xdp.attach.fallback_generic` carrying the iface name, then retry with generic flags. KPI K1 emission point. Flips S-2.2-02 RED → GREEN.
acceptance_criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
Loader tries native attach first, generic on documented fallback errors
Single structured warn event emitted with stable name + iface field
Test asserts warn captured via tracing-subscriber test layer
Zero silent fall-throughs (per Slice 01 KPI)

steps.name: Lift xdp_pass + PACKET_COUNTER to real veth pair
criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
Test creates veth0/veth1 via ip link, attaches xdp_pass to veth0, pushes 100 frames from veth1, asserts PACKET_COUNTER == 100 via bpftool map dump
steps.description: Re-target Phase 2.1's xdp_pass + PACKET_COUNTER LruHashMap to a real veth pair. Adds veth-create/teardown helpers in `tests/integration/veth_attach.rs` (root-required; runs via `cargo xtask lima run --` on macOS). Flips starting scenario S-2.2-01 GREEN end-to-end and unblocks Slice 02.
acceptance_criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
veth pair helpers create/teardown idempotently with CAP_NET_ADMIN
100 ETH+IPv4 frames pushed from veth1 produce PACKET_COUNTER == 100
Counter read via real `bpftool map dump name PACKET_COUNTER`
Integration test gated `integration-tests` per .claude/rules/testing.md
Phase 2.1 lo-attach regression test still passes
steps.risk_mitigation: DISCUSS Risk #6 — lima-vs-CI parity; helpers bail on EPERM with skip-message; CI runs ubuntu-latest with sudo wrap

phases.name: Slice 02 — SERVICE_MAP forward path (single VIP)
phases.description: Land xdp_service_map_lookup with single-VIP BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH lookup, DNAT rewrite, IP+L4 checksum recompute, XDP_TX. Graduate EbpfDataplane::update_service from Ok(()) stub to real single-entry insert/remove via typed ServiceMapHandle. Lands S-2.2-04..08. Migrate SimDataplane HashMap → BTreeMap (deterministic iteration prerequisite for slices 03–08). Implements ADR-0040 (three-map split foundation). Tier 3 phases (02–06) require cargo xtask bpf-build to produce target/xtask/bpf-objects/overdrive_bpf.o before integration tests link, per ADR-0038 § 4. The xtask infrastructure already triggers this, but contributors running --features integration-tests outside the xtask wrapper must invoke bpf-build first (cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask bpf-build on macOS).
acceptance: S-2.2-04 Tier 2 — PROG_TEST_RUN: SERVICE_MAP hit returns XDP_TX with rewritten IP/port + valid checksums
S-2.2-05 Tier 2 — empty SERVICE_MAP returns XDP_PASS, no rewrite
S-2.2-06 Tier 3 — 10 TCP SYNs forwarded end-to-end across veth (tcpdump capture)
S-2.2-07 Tier 4 — veristat baseline recorded under perf-baseline/main/verifier-budget/, ≤ 50% of 1M ceiling
S-2.2-08 Tier 2 — truncated IPv4 (10-byte header) returns XDP_PASS, no crash, no SERVICE_MAP lookup

steps.name: ServiceVip + ServiceId newtypes; SimDataplane BTreeMap migration
criteria: ServiceVip and ServiceId STRICT newtypes in overdrive-core (FromStr/Display/serde/rkyv/proptest)
SimDataplane internal HashMap → BTreeMap
deterministic-iteration property test passes
steps.description: Add the two STRICT newtypes Slice 02 references (case-insensitive parse, structured ParseError, rkyv archive roundtrip proptest, FromStr/Display match, serde matches FromStr). Migrate SimDataplane's service-table HashMap to BTreeMap so iteration order is deterministic across DST seeds — prerequisite for every later slice's invariants. Per development.md § Ordered-collection choice.
acceptance_criteria: ServiceVip parses host:port:proto canonical form, lowercase Display
ServiceId parses opaque string ID, structured ParseError on invalid
Both pass FromStr/Display/serde/rkyv proptest roundtrips
SimDataplane services field is BTreeMap<ServiceId, ServiceEntry>
Deterministic iteration test holds across 1024 proptest seeds

steps.name: ServiceMapHandle typed wrapper + EbpfDataplane::update_service body
criteria: ServiceMapHandle wraps aya HashMap
exposes only insert(ServiceVip, Backend) / remove(ServiceVip)
update_service performs single-entry insert/remove for a single VIP
Tier 1 unit test covers handle round-trip
steps.description: Replace the raw `aya::maps::HashMap` call site with a typed `ServiceMapHandle` newtype in overdrive-dataplane. Constructor accepts host-endian inputs, converts to network-endian on write (architecture.md §11 endianness lockstep). Graduate `EbpfDataplane::update_service` from `Ok(())` no-op to a real single-entry insert/remove against the handle. Sets up Slice 03's atomic-swap step.
acceptance_criteria: ServiceMapHandle exposes insert/remove only — raw HashMap not visible at call site
Userspace writes host-endian; bytes on the map are network-endian
Userspace proptest round-trips host-order writes against host-order reads
EbpfDataplane::update_service inserts/removes in real BPF map (Tier 3 confirms)
steps.risk_mitigation: DISCUSS Risk #4 — endianness mismatch caught by proptest at userspace boundary; Tier 2 + Tier 3 lockstep follows in steps 02-03 / 02-04

steps.name: xdp_service_map_lookup program + PROG_TEST_RUN triptych
criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
xdp_service_map_lookup parses ETH/IPv4/TCP/UDP, looks up ServiceKey in SERVICE_MAP, rewrites dest IP+port on hit (XDP_TX) or returns XDP_PASS
PKTGEN/SETUP/CHECK Tier 2 covers hit + miss + truncated frame
steps.description: Land the kernel-side XDP program in `crates/overdrive-bpf/src/programs/service_map.rs` per ADR-0040. Recompute IP and L4 checksums via `bpf_l3_csum_replace` / `bpf_l4_csum_replace`. Tier 2 triptych asserts hit returns XDP_TX with rewritten headers, miss returns XDP_PASS, truncated frame returns XDP_PASS without crash. Flips S-2.2-04, S-2.2-05, S-2.2-08 GREEN.
acceptance_criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
Hit case: action XDP_TX, dest IP/port rewritten, checksums valid
Miss case: action XDP_PASS, no rewrite
Truncated IPv4 case: action XDP_PASS, no crash, no SERVICE_MAP lookup
Tier 2 PROG_TEST_RUN map state cleared between sub-tests by default
verifier accepts the program on each kernel in the matrix
steps.risk_mitigation: DISCUSS Risk #1 — verifier complexity quantified in 02-05 baseline; ADR-0040 chooses bpf_*_csum_replace to keep verifier walks tight

steps.name: Tier 3 veth forwarding test (10 TCP SYNs)
criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating

Tier 3 test attaches xdp_service_map_lookup to veth0, populates SERVICE_MAP via update_service, sends 10 TCP SYNs to VIP, captures via tcpdump, asserts every frame leaves veth1 with rewritten dest + valid checksums
steps.description: End-to-end veth integration test that proves the kernel-loaded program forwards real packets. Uses pnet to craft TCP SYNs and tokio-tun to drive them. tcpdump capture validates wire-side rewrite. Flips S-2.2-06 GREEN.
acceptance_criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
10/10 frames leave veth1 with dest 10.1.0.5:9000
IP and TCP checksums validate against scapy/pnet recomputation
All 10 returned actions are XDP_TX
Test runs via `cargo xtask lima run --` on macOS

steps.name: Record veristat baseline for xdp_service_map_lookup
criteria: perf-baseline/main/verifier-budget/veristat-service-map.txt records instruction count for compiled xdp_service_map_lookup.o
count ≤ 50% of 1M-privileged ceiling
checked into repo
steps.description: Run veristat against the compiled program and persist the baseline file under `perf-baseline/main/verifier-budget/`. KPI K7 first emission point. Slice 07's gate enforces deltas against this; the file IS the assertion. No xtask wiring yet — that lands in Phase 7.
acceptance_criteria: Baseline file committed under perf-baseline/main/verifier-budget/
Recorded instruction count ≤ 500_000 (50% of ceiling)
veristat output format matches Slice 07 gate parser shape
Baseline-update commit message documents the kernel + commit SHA
steps.mutation_target: skip-rationale: baseline file is a generated artifact with no operator sites; mutants empty-filter is a vacuous pass

phases.name: Slice 03 — HASH_OF_MAPS atomic per-service backend swap
phases.description: Restructure SERVICE_MAP from BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH to BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH_OF_MAPS (outer ServiceId, inner ARRAY of BackendId, inner size 256 per ADR-0040 Q5=A). Add flat BACKEND_MAP keyed by BackendId. Implement EbpfDataplane::update_service 5-step atomic swap with single 64-bit pointer update + orphan GC. Lands S-2.2-09..11. Random-slot lookup is intentional simplicity — Slice 04 replaces it with Maglev. Implements ADR-0040 (HASH_OF_MAPS atomic swap + checksum + DropClass split).
acceptance: S-2.2-09 Tier 3 — atomic swap under 50 kpps (CI) / 100 kpps (Lima) drops zero packets; post-swap distribution within ±10% even
S-2.2-10 Tier 3 — removing a backend purges orphaned BACKEND_MAP entries after GC pass
S-2.2-11 Tier 3 — kernel-rejected inner-map allocation surfaces DataplaneError::MapAllocFailed; existing inner map preserved

steps.name: BackendId newtype + BACKEND_MAP + DST atomic-swap invariant
criteria: BackendId STRICT newtype in overdrive-core
BACKEND_MAP declared as BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH
SimDataplane mirrors atomic swap (single mutex, BTreeMap reassignment)
BackendSetSwapAtomic invariant in overdrive-sim::invariants always holds
steps.description: Add BackendId newtype (FromStr/Display/serde/rkyv/proptest). Declare BACKEND_MAP in overdrive-bpf. Mirror the swap shape in SimDataplane as a single mutex acquisition wrapping a `BTreeMap` reassignment so DST replay matches production. Add the BackendSetSwapAtomic always-invariant to overdrive-sim::invariants and wire into Invariant::ALL.
acceptance_criteria: BackendId newtype completeness covered by proptest
BACKEND_MAP declared with key BackendId, value Backend { ip, port, weight, flags }
SimDataplane::update_service performs one BTreeMap reassignment under one mutex
BackendSetSwapAtomic invariant: every observation sees pre- or post-swap, never mixed
Invariant added to overdrive-sim::invariants::Invariant::ALL

steps.name: HASH_OF_MAPS restructure + EbpfDataplane 5-step atomic swap
criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
SERVICE_MAP restructured to BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH_OF_MAPS (inner ARRAY size 256)
update_service implements 5-step swap (BACKEND_MAP upsert → fresh inner → outer pointer update → orphan GC → release)
MapAllocFailed surfaced on kernel rejection
existing inner map preserved on failure
steps.description: Restructure SERVICE_MAP per Cilium reference (research §2.1, §3.3). Implement the 5-step atomic swap in EbpfDataplane::update_service. Step 3 is the load-bearing single 64-bit pointer update. On kernel rejection of inner-map alloc (ENOMEM/EPERM) return DataplaneError::MapAllocFailed and leave the existing inner map untouched. Flips S-2.2-11 GREEN.
acceptance_criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
SERVICE_MAP outer = HASH_OF_MAPS keyed by ServiceId, inner = ARRAY of BackendId size 256
update_service performs 5 steps in order; step 3 is a single bpf_map_update_elem
Inner-map alloc failure returns DataplaneError::MapAllocFailed
Pre-existing inner map unchanged on failure
Tier 3 confirms via subsequent traffic still forwarding to old set
steps.risk_mitigation: DISCUSS Risk #1 / #5 — verifier complexity for HASH_OF_MAPS NULL-check; ADR-0040 mandates inner-pointer NULL-check after lookup; xtask verifier-regress at 02-05 catches regressions early

steps.name: Zero-drop atomic-swap test + BACKEND_MAP orphan GC
criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
Tier 3 atomic_swap test: xdp-trafficgen at 50 kpps (CI) / 100 kpps (Lima) sustains 5s
trigger backend-set swap from {B1} to {B1,B2,B3}
assert send count == receive count (zero drops)
post-swap distribution ±10% even
subsequent test: removing B3 + GC pass leaves no orphan in BACKEND_MAP
steps.description: Drives the slice's K3 KPI: zero-drop atomic swap. Measurement source is xdp-trafficgen send count vs sink receive count (DROP_COUNTER lands in Slice 06). Companion GC test asserts that BACKEND_MAP carries no IDs unreferenced by any service after a removal+GC pass. Flips S-2.2-09, S-2.2-10 GREEN.
acceptance_criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
Zero packet drops across the swap window at sustained kpps
Post-swap backend distribution within ±10% even (random-slot baseline; Slice 04 tightens with Maglev)
BACKEND_MAP orphan GC removes IDs with no service reference
Tier 3 test covers both swap and GC paths via fixture
steps.risk_mitigation: DISCUSS Risk #3 — kpps ceiling on ubuntu-latest; gate at send-count==receive-count rather than absolute pps; Lima dev box runs higher rate as confirmation

phases.name: Slice 04 — Weighted Maglev consistent hashing
phases.description: Replace Slice 03's random slot selection with Maglev consistent hashing inside MAGLEV_MAP (HASH_OF_MAPS, inner ARRAY size M=16381 per ADR-0041 Q6=A). MaglevTableSize STRICT newtype constrained to Cilium prime list. Pure userspace `maglev::generate(BTreeMap<BackendId,Weight>, MaglevTableSize) → Vec<BackendId>` ships with weighted Eisenbud permutation (multiplicity ∝ weight). XDP lookup chain switches to MAGLEV_MAP-indexed. Lands S-2.2-12..16. Implements ADR-0041 (weighted Maglev + endianness lockstep).
acceptance: S-2.2-12 Tier 1 proptest — maglev::generate is deterministic across 1024 seeds (bit-identical)
S-2.2-13 Tier 1 proptest — single-backend removal among 100 shifts ≤ 2% of 100k flows (1% forced + ≤1% incidental)
S-2.2-14 Tier 4 — verifier accepts post-Maglev program; instruction count ≤ 50% of 1M ceiling; baseline updated
S-2.2-15 Tier 1 proptest — ±5% even distribution under equal weights
S-2.2-16 Tier 1 proptest — ±2% honoring of declared weights under skewed weights

steps.name: MaglevTableSize newtype constrained to Cilium prime list
criteria: MaglevTableSize STRICT newtype in overdrive-core
structured ParseError on others
full Display/serde/rkyv/proptest roundtrip
steps.description: STRICT newtype that makes invalid M values unrepresentable. FromStr/TryFrom<u32> reject any value not in the Cilium prime list with a structured `ParseError::InvalidMaglevTableSize { provided, allowed }` variant. Default constant `MaglevTableSize::DEFAULT_16381`. Per development.md § Newtypes — STRICT by default.
acceptance_criteria: FromStr rejects 17 (composite) and 13 (not in list) with structured ParseError
FromStr accepts every prime in the Cilium list (case-insensitive)
Display/FromStr/serde/rkyv roundtrip proptest passes 1024 cases
DEFAULT_16381 constant exposed for the M=16381 default

steps.name: Pure maglev::generate (Eisenbud permutation, weighted multiplicity)
criteria: Pure sync `maglev::generate(&BTreeMap<BackendId, Weight>, MaglevTableSize) -> Vec<BackendId>` ships in overdrive-dataplane
bit-identical across runs given same inputs
weighted variant expands per-backend slot multiplicity proportional to weight
saturating arithmetic on overflow
steps.description: Userspace pure function implementing Eisenbud's NSDI 2016 Maglev permutation algorithm with weighted multiplicity expansion. BTreeMap input ordering keeps output deterministic across nodes (per development.md § Ordered-collection choice). Saturating arithmetic on weight × multiplicity. Flips S-2.2-12 GREEN as Tier 1 proptest.
acceptance_criteria: Two successive calls with identical inputs return bit-identical Vec<BackendId>
Equal weights produce ±5% even distribution across M slots
Skewed weights honored within ±2% of declared distribution
Saturating arithmetic on overflow boundary (proptest covers boundary)
Pure sync function: no I/O, no global state, no Instant::now
steps.risk_mitigation: DISCUSS Risk #2 — weighted-Maglev component count; ADR-0041 cites Eisenbud reference impl as the precedent

steps.name: Disruption-bound proptest (≤2% on N=100, M=16381)
criteria: Tier 1 proptest: any seeded set of 100 equally-weighted backends + 100k 5-tuple flows
remove one backend
assert ≤2% total flow shift (1% forced + ≤1% incidental per Maglev's published bound)
steps.description: Property test asserting Maglev's disruption bound — the structural reason consistent hashing matters for canary deployments. Uses seeded Entropy per development.md § Port-trait dependencies. Flips S-2.2-13 GREEN.
acceptance_criteria: ≤1% incidental shift on flows that were NOT on the evicted backend
1% forced shift on flows that WERE on the evicted backend
Total ≤2% across 100k-flow population
Property holds across 1024 proptest seeds
DST invariant MaglevDistributionEven added to Invariant::ALL

steps.name: MAGLEV_MAP + XDP lookup switch to MAGLEV_MAP-indexed
criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
MAGLEV_MAP declared as BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH_OF_MAPS (inner ARRAY size 16381)
XDP program lookup chain: hash 5-tuple → modulo M → MAGLEV_MAP lookup → BackendId → BACKEND_MAP → backend address → DNAT rewrite + XDP_TX
EbpfDataplane::update_service regenerates Maglev table on every backend-set change and swaps via Slice 03's 5-step shape
steps.description: Replace Slice 03's random-slot lookup with MAGLEV_MAP-indexed lookup in the kernel program. Userspace regenerates the Maglev permutation on every backend-set change and writes the inner ARRAY before the atomic outer-pointer swap. Maglev table generation is a userspace concern; the kernel program does only the lookup.
acceptance_criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
MAGLEV_MAP declared with inner ARRAY size 16381 (default)
XDP program switches from random slot to MAGLEV_MAP indexing
update_service regenerates Maglev table; Slice 03 5-step swap unchanged
Tier 3 confirmation: real xdp-trafficgen distribution holds within ±5%
steps.risk_mitigation: DISCUSS Risk #1 / #2 — verifier complexity for MAGLEV_MAP lookup; veristat baseline update in 04-05 quantifies it; rollback path is reverting to Slice 03's random-slot

steps.name: Update veristat baseline for post-Maglev program
criteria: perf-baseline/main/verifier-budget/veristat-service-map.txt updated post-Maglev
instruction count ≤ 50% of 1M ceiling
commit message documents kernel + commit SHA + delta vs Slice 02 baseline
steps.description: Re-record the veristat baseline so Slice 07's gate enforces against the post-Maglev shape. Closes the slice's K7 KPI. Flips S-2.2-14 GREEN.
acceptance_criteria: Updated baseline file committed
Instruction count ≤ 500_000 (50% of ceiling)
Commit message documents kernel version, commit SHA, delta vs Slice 02
Companion DST invariant MaglevDeterministic added to Invariant::ALL

phases.name: Slice 05 — REVERSE_NAT (TC egress + endianness lockstep)
phases.description: Add REVERSE_NAT_MAP (HASH, key BackendKey {ip,port,proto}, value Vip) — third map of the Cilium three-map split. Land tc_reverse_nat TC-egress program (ADR-0041 Q2=A: TC egress over XDP egress) that rewrites source IP/port back to VIP on response packets. EbpfDataplane::update_service writes/removes REVERSE_NAT entries in lockstep with service-backend changes. Lands S-2.2-17..20. Parallel-able with Slice 06. Implements ADR-0041 (TC egress + endianness lockstep + REVERSE_NAT).
acceptance: S-2.2-17 Tier 3 — real `nc -l 9000` ↔ `nc 10.0.0.1 8080` TCP connection completes 3-way handshake + payload echo
S-2.2-18 Tier 2 — PROG_TEST_RUN: tc_reverse_nat hit rewrites source to VIP with valid checksums
S-2.2-19 Tier 2 + Tier 1 proptest — wire-order packet produces host-order ReverseKey matching userspace seed
S-2.2-20 Tier 3 — removed backend's REVERSE_NAT entry purged; late response treated as non-LB (no rewrite)

steps.name: REVERSE_NAT_MAP + ReverseNatLockstep DST invariant
criteria: REVERSE_NAT_MAP declared as BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH (key BackendKey, value Vip)
SimDataplane mirrors map
ReverseNatLockstep always-invariant: every forward-path SERVICE_MAP entry has matching REVERSE_NAT entry
removing a backend purges both
steps.description: Add the third map and the lockstep DST invariant. Lockstep rule: update_service writes/removes REVERSE_NAT entries atomically with SERVICE_MAP changes. Sim mirror is a BTreeMap keyed by BackendKey with the lockstep enforced inside the same mutex acquisition that wraps SERVICE_MAP updates.
acceptance_criteria: REVERSE_NAT_MAP declared in overdrive-bpf
BackendKey newtype completeness (ip + port + proto, host-endian)
ReverseNatLockstep invariant always holds across DST seeds
Sim writes/removes REVERSE_NAT in lockstep with SERVICE_MAP under one mutex
Invariant added to Invariant::ALL

steps.name: tc_reverse_nat TC-egress program + PROG_TEST_RUN triptych
criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
tc_reverse_nat program at crates/overdrive-bpf/src/programs/reverse_nat.rs
parses ETH/IPv4/TCP|UDP, looks up BackendKey in REVERSE_NAT_MAP, rewrites source IP/port to VIP on hit (TC_ACT_OK), passes on miss
PKTGEN/SETUP/CHECK Tier 2 covers hit case
steps.description: TC-egress hook (ADR-0041 Q2=A) rather than XDP egress — BPF_PROG_TEST_RUN supports TC programs. Recompute IP/L4 checksums via bpf_l3/l4_csum_replace. Flips S-2.2-18 GREEN.
acceptance_criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
TC egress program parses ETH+IPv4+TCP|UDP
Hit: TC_ACT_OK, source rewritten to VIP, checksums valid
Miss: TC_ACT_OK, no rewrite (non-LB pass-through)
Tier 2 PROG_TEST_RUN covers both branches
steps.risk_mitigation: DISCUSS Risk #4 — endianness lockstep at TC boundary; addressed by 05-03's wire-order proptest

steps.name: Endianness-lockstep wire-order ↔ host-order ReverseKey proptest
criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
Tier 2 + userspace proptest: synthetic IPv4+TCP packet with known wire-order bytes through reverse_key_from_packet produces host-order ReverseKey matching userspace seed
userspace proptest round-trips host-order writes against host-order reads (no userspace endian flip)
steps.description: Closes architecture.md §11 endianness lockstep guarantee. Two-sided test: kernel-side reverse_key_from_packet helper + userspace ReverseNatMapHandle that writes/reads host-endian. Flips S-2.2-19 GREEN.
acceptance_criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
reverse_key_from_packet at kernel boundary returns host-order ReverseKey
ReverseKey matches userspace-seeded host-order key bit-for-bit
Userspace proptest covers 1024 wire-order packet shapes
ReverseNatMapHandle write→read roundtrip is bit-identical
steps.risk_mitigation: DISCUSS Risk #4 — endianness mismatch at the wire boundary; lockstep proptest is the mechanical defence

payload echoes

acceptance_criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating

phases.name: Slice 06 — Pre-SERVICE_MAP packet-shape sanity prologue
phases.description: Prepend a 5-check Cloudflare-order sanity prologue (EtherType → IP version+IHL → IP total_length → protocol → TCP flags) to BOTH xdp_service_map_lookup and xdp_reverse_nat via a shared `#[inline(always)]` helper (ADR-0040 Q3=C). DROP_COUNTER (PERCPU_ARRAY, 6 slots per Q7) and typed DropClass enum in overdrive-core. Lands S-2.2-21..25. Parallel-able with Slice 05. Implements ADR-0040 (sanity prologue + 6 DropClass slots).
acceptance: S-2.2-21 Tier 2 — truncated IPv4 (IHL=4) drops with DROP_COUNTER[MalformedHeader] += 1; SERVICE_MAP NOT consulted
S-2.2-22 Tier 2 — pathological TCP flags (SYN+RST) drops with DROP_COUNTER[MalformedHeader] += 1
S-2.2-23 Tier 2 — IPv6 (EtherType 0x86DD) returns XDP_PASS, no DROP_COUNTER increment
S-2.2-24 Tier 3 — mixed batch increments per-class counters correctly (50 valid + 10 truncated + 10 SYN+RST + 10 IPv6)
S-2.2-25 Tier 4 — verifier-budget delta vs Slice 04 baseline ≤ 20%; absolute ≤ 60% of 1M ceiling

steps.name: DropClass enum + DROP_COUNTER PERCPU_ARRAY (6 slots)
criteria: DropClass enum in overdrive-core (Reserved, MalformedHeader, UnknownVip, BackendUnreachable, PolicyDeny, RateLimit) maps 1:1 to slot indices 0..5
DROP_COUNTER declared as BPF_MAP_TYPE_PERCPU_ARRAY size 6
SimDataplane mirrors counter
steps.description: Add the typed DropClass enum (6 slots per ADR-0040 Q7=A) and the PERCPU_ARRAY map. Slot 0 reserved (drift detection), slots 1–5 carry the named classes. Per-CPU avoids cross-CPU contention; userspace aggregator sums across CPUs. Per development.md § Newtypes — STRICT.
acceptance_criteria: DropClass enum with explicit u32 discriminants (0..5)
Slot index drift compile-fail test in trybuild
DROP_COUNTER size 6 (Reserved + 5 classes)
Per-CPU aggregator sums correctly under proptest
DropClass added to overdrive-core public API

steps.name: Shared #[inline(always)] sanity prologue helper
criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
Sanity-prologue helper at crates/overdrive-bpf/src/programs/sanity.rs marked #[inline(always)]
5 Cloudflare checks (EtherType / IP version+IHL / IP total_length / proto / TCP flags)
returns Result<ContinueToServiceMap, XdpAction>
invoked from both xdp_service_map_lookup and tc_reverse_nat
steps.description: ADR-0040 Q3=C decision: shared helper rather than duplicated inline code, with #[inline(always)] so the verifier sees the same instruction stream in both call sites. Five-check order matches Cloudflare's published order (research §7.2). Non-IPv4 / non-TCP-or-UDP returns XDP_PASS (LB is not a firewall).
acceptance_criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating
Helper marked #[inline(always)]
Invoked from xdp_service_map_lookup and tc_reverse_nat
Five checks in Cloudflare order; first failure short-circuits
Non-IPv4 + non-TCP/UDP cases return XDP_PASS (Tier 2 confirms)
steps.risk_mitigation: DISCUSS Risk #1 / #5 — verifier delta budget; quantified by 06-05 baseline; rollback to inline duplication if budget breached

steps.name: Tier 2 PROG_TEST_RUN: malformed header + TCP flags + IPv6 pass
criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating

acceptance_criteria: Test compiles with #![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")] entrypoint, runs via cargo nextest run --features integration-tests (cargo xtask lima run -- on macOS) per .claude/rules/testing.md § Integration vs unit gating

phases.name: Slice 07 — Tier 4 perf gates land on main

Implements ADR-0038 D8 (`// TODO(#29): wire when first real program lands`).

phases.name: Slice 08 — SERVICE_MAP hydrator reconciler (J-PLAT-004 ESR closure)
phases.description: Add ServiceMapHydrator reconciler at crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconcilers/service_map_hydrator.rs per ADR-0042. Sync reconcile per ADR-0035/0036; runtime owns hydration of intent + observation + View. Adds Action::DataplaneUpdateService variant + service_hydration_results ObservationStore table (additive LWW). Two ESR DST invariants + dst-lint enforcement. Lands S-2.2-28..30. Parallel-able with Slices 03–07. Implements ADR-0042 (ServiceMapHydrator + Action::DataplaneUpdateService + service_hydration_results).
acceptance: S-2.2-28 Tier 1 — HydratorEventuallyConverges: actual.fingerprint == desired.fingerprint within bounded ticks across every DST seed
S-2.2-29 Tier 1 — Action shim writes service_hydration_results Completed row on dispatch; next tick observes convergence
S-2.2-30 Tier 1 — HydratorIdempotentSteadyState + retry-budget proptest + ReconcilerIsPure invariant + dst-lint clean (no .await / no Instant::now / no DB handle in reconcile)

steps.name: Action::DataplaneUpdateService + service_hydration_results table + action_shim modulisation
criteria: Action::DataplaneUpdateService variant added in crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler.rs
action shim arm flips from todo!() to real dispatch
service_hydration_results table added in overdrive-control-plane/src/observation_store/ via additive LWW migration
action_shim.rs converted from single file to directory module with action_shim_service_hydration.rs sibling promoted in
steps.description: Cross-cutting plumbing for the hydrator. New typed Action variant, additive ObservationStore table for hydration results (status: Pending/InFlight/Completed{fingerprint,applied_at}/Failed), and the directory-modulisation of action_shim.rs that the DISTILL agent flagged. Action shim dispatches against Arc<dyn Dataplane> so production wires EbpfDataplane and DST wires SimDataplane. The `BackendSetFingerprint = u64` alias and `fingerprint(vip, backends) -> BackendSetFingerprint` rkyv-archived helper land here, consumed by the hydrator State construction in the next step.
acceptance_criteria: Action::DataplaneUpdateService { service_id, vip, backends, correlation } in reconciler.rs
Match arm in action shim flips from todo!() to real Dataplane::update_service dispatch
service_hydration_results LWW table added (additive migration; rejoin guard per §4)
action_shim.rs → action_shim/ directory module with sibling files moved in
Tier 1 dispatch test asserts Completed row written on Ok(())
steps.risk_mitigation: DISCUSS Risk #7 — additive-only schema migration discipline (§4 Consistency Guardrails); two-phase rollout codified by the migration helper; tombstone sweep covered by existing reconciler

steps.name: ServiceMapHydrator reconciler (sync reconcile, ESR pair invariants)
criteria: ServiceMapHydrator at crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconcilers/service_map_hydrator.rs
sync reconcile per ADR-0035/0036 (no .await, no Instant::now, no DB handle)
typed State (desired/actual per ServiceId) and View (attempts, last_failure_seen_at, last-seen service_backends generation per service)
HydratorEventuallyConverges + HydratorIdempotentSteadyState invariants in overdrive-sim::invariants always pass across DST seeds
steps.description: The reconciler itself. State projects desired backends from IntentStore-derived projection and actual from service_backends ObservationStore rows. View carries inputs only — attempts, last_failure_seen_at, last-seen generation — never derived deadlines (per development.md § Persist inputs, not derived state). Reconcile is pure-sync: emits Action::DataplaneUpdateService when fingerprints diverge. Flips S-2.2-28 GREEN.
acceptance_criteria: Reconciler implements Reconciler trait per ADR-0035 (sync reconcile)
State and View are typed; View derives Serialize/Deserialize/Default/Clone
View carries inputs only — no next_attempt_at field
HydratorEventuallyConverges (eventual) holds across every DST seed
HydratorIdempotentSteadyState (always) holds across every DST seed
ReconcilerIsPure invariant continues to pass with hydrator added
steps.risk_mitigation: DISCUSS Risk #8 — ESR convergence under transient gossip; View carries last-seen generation so the reconciler tolerates seconds of staleness; backoff via persisted inputs prevents thrash

steps.name: Retry-budget proptest + dst-lint enforcement of reconciler purity
criteria: Tier 1 proptest: hydrator emits Action iff tick.now_unix >= last_failure_seen_at + backoff_for_attempt(attempts)
deadline recomputed every tick from inputs (not persisted)
dst-lint scans service_map_hydrator.rs and rejects any .await / Instant::now / SystemTime::now / DB handle in reconcile body
steps.description: Final closure. Property test asserts the retry-budget shape from development.md § Persist inputs, not derived state. dst-lint mechanical check enforces reconciler purity at PR time. Flips S-2.2-30 GREEN and closes US-08.
acceptance_criteria: Retry proptest: no Action within backoff window; Action re-emitted at window boundary
View RetryMemory unchanged within backoff window
dst-lint scanner walks reconcile body for forbidden constructs
PR fails if .await / Instant::now / SystemTime::now / DB handle appears in reconcile
ReconcilerIsPure DST invariant continues to pass
